results.label: Standard — memoir author on a writing podcast
results.output_summary: Three sections plus overflow. Opening: 3 warm-up questions about her path to memoir. Core: 8 questions with follow-up prompts, centered on the ethics of writing about family. Includes 'Your mother didn't speak to you for three months after reading the manuscript — what was the first conversation after that silence?' with NOTE about sensitivity and alternate framing. Closing: 3 questions. Overflow: 4 questions. No yes/no questions. Next Step: lead with question 1 about what memoir writing costs personally.

A1.note: Opening (3), Core (8 with follow-ups), Closing (3), Overflow (4). Correct structure.

A2.note: No yes/no questions. Follow-ups are specific: 'If she says the relationship recovered, push on what was permanently changed.'

A3.note: Mother-silence question flagged with NOTE. Next Step identifies opening question.

results.label: Minimal — only required fields
results.output_summary: Three sections plus overflow. Opening: 4 questions about his transition from cooking to consulting. Core: 7 questions about food safety truths. Closing: 3 questions. Overflow: 4. All open-ended. Tone matches casual conversational register. No sensitive topics flagged (none specified).

A1.note: Correct structure with all sections. Core has follow-up prompts.

A2.note: No yes/no questions. Follow-ups specific to food safety topics.

A3.note: No sensitive topics to flag. Next Step present with opening recommendation.

results.label: European context — Dutch urban planner on a cities podcast
results.output_summary: Three sections plus overflow. Opening: 3 questions about what drew him to urban planning. Core: 8 questions about why copying Dutch infrastructure fails, with European city examples (Copenhagen, Barcelona, Paris). NOTE on the political controversy topic-to-avoid. Follow-ups specific: 'If he says it's about political will, push on which European city has come closest and what they did differently.' Closing: 4 questions. Overflow: 5. Next Step: lead with question about what visitors to Dutch cities misunderstand about the infrastructure.

A1.note: All sections present. Core has 8 questions with detailed follow-ups. Overflow section present.

A2.note: No yes/no questions. Follow-ups reference specific European cities and policy contexts.

A3.note: Political controversy NOTE flagged. Next Step recommends opening question and guest-research-brief.
